title: Include hours in Timeline selection
excerpt:
We love that we can now include the hour in a deadline/due date. But we would love to have that ability also for the timeline start and end dates/times. 
As an example, we need to track the times that our researchers are…

posts.author: Glenda (Glenda Bishop)
posts.cookedHtml:
We love that we can now include the hour in a deadline/due date. But we would love to have that ability also for the timeline start and end dates/times.

As an example, we need to track the times that our researchers are out doing interviews. This is partly for project flow, but also for staff safety so that we know when to expect to finish since they are going into people’s homes. Since an interview happens on a single day, timeline on a day basis isn’t sufficient to know when they will be in and out. Also, if they have multiple interviews in a day, we can’t see which order they occur in. So having start and end times within a day would be really helpful for planning interviews and tracking staff.

I’m sure this would also be helpful for task management when planning discrete tasks that need to happen at certain times in other projects, sort of like a mini calendar. And depending on the timeline/calendar view used, you could see an overview on a day-to-day basis or get more granular for an hour-to-hour basis.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Glenda,

Welcome to the community

Thanks for the feedback - I think you’re asking for an hourly view of the timeline and that’s something we’ve heard from other users!

We’re going to be making some improvements for resource planning in the near future so I do think this is one of the features which will be considered as part of those. Stay tuned for updates!

In the meantime, does a combination of the date column with time and the calendar view help?

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Love this idea for an hourly timeline option and glad to hear it’s on the plans @Julia-monday.com. For us it would be great in partnership with the dependency columns for our event days. Would allow us to ensure the agenda is well organised and timed well.

Glenda:

I’m sure this would also be helpful for task management when planning discrete tasks that need to happen at certain times in other projects, sort of like a mini calendar.

posts.author: Glenda (Glenda Bishop)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are currently using the date column with time and the calendar view to manage this. The catch is that with this approach it only shows up as a 1/2 hour block, yet our interviews can go for anywhere from 1-2 hours, so it doesn’t fully demonstrate the time commitment for the task.

As a workaround, we’ve added two additional hour columns for Start and Finish, so that in Table view we can see how long they will be at the interviews. But it’s clunky this way so a timeline hourly view would be much better.

posts.author: igriboff (Ilan Griboff)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com Any updates on the roadmap for this? For Resource management this is a critical feature for us

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @igriboff! Brett here

Just to make sure I am aligned on the functionality you need as well, as you looking for end times on a date column or timeline as well?

I know our team is working on adding the end time for date columns, which would allow you to choose blocks of time for specific tasks!

Would love any more info you can provide on your workflow/feedback.

Cheers!

posts.author: Tobias (Tobias Wiesendanger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Brett

We need to be able to plan task by time (in hours not just time). Our task will be linked to a starting and an endtime.

Also we need a bidirectional way to sync task to outlook and get events from outlook so we dont plan tasks in monday over time already booked in outlook.

In the end we would like to get a way better projectmanagment workflow, workload overview and reporting for things like completion, projects that are overdue or not touched for xy days.

I evaluated many tools and found none that can do that, not even jira. I would love to use monday, but that is a dealbreaker. If it helps im happy to provide more infos by a teamsmeeting or whatever tool you prefer.

I can probably show you way more in detail how we work. My english is not the best but it should be enough to talk you trough.

regards

Tobias

posts.author: igriboff (Ilan Griboff)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brett,
End time on the date column would work for us as well!

Looking forward to it

posts.author: Mel (Mel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tobias,

Did you find a solution to this as this is our issue as well.

Thanks

posts.author: tegan.owen (Tegan Owen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Just wanted to say we would also appreciate this feature.

We current use the time on the date column and have two date column, one form start date and time and another for end date and time. But it doesn’t allow is to sync with our calendars so it creates a bit of a mess when items change on the calendar but not on monday.com or when it changes on monday.com and not the calendar.

Thanks

posts.author: djbeame (Bernard Wilson)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great inclusion. Has there been any more progress on this? This would be particularly handy for run sheets for the events industry (our industry).

posts.author: IvanH (Ivan H)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com this is a really crucial feature for us to be able to schedule engineers on an hourly basis. The timeline column is great, it would be useful even if you were able to add the same time functionality to that as the date column. It would be really appreciated!!

posts.author: jgiordan (Jessica Giordano)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be extremely useful for us as well - adding both a start and end time as well as start and end dates.

posts.author: tegan.owen (Tegan Owen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just want to second that the end time on the date column would also work really well for us.

Any news on how long away this feature is?

posts.author: tanja (tanja)
posts.cookedHtml:
That would be a nice feature.

Also, it would be nice to be able to estimate tasks in hours, and that they will be displayed in the google calendar at that time so that not all tasks needs to be days. I’ve got lots of small tasks during the day, and in my google calendar it’s just stacked at the top and not very easy to overview.
It would be nice too, if I change a time in the google calendar, that it changes in monday, so I can move my tasks around.
Lastly, tasks done should not be shown in the google calendar.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
brett-monday.com:

I know our team is working on adding the end time for date columns, which would allow you to choose blocks of time for specific tasks!

This is great news @brett-monday.com

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
tanja:

Lastly, tasks done should not be shown in the google calendar.

Yes, totally agree. I find myself quite often clicking on tasks in the agenda that already have a Done status.
What helped me was to use an automation to change the date column to ‘now’ when status is set to Done. No more Done tasks in the future but this automation isn’t desired in some cases.

Two way syncing with the calendar would be awesome offcourse

posts.author: sistemas.arquiara (Diana P Murcia R)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news on this front? We also need to program blocks of hours and the 1/2 hour its just not working to sync with a real calendar.

posts.author: bhutch (Bill Hutchinson)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes - It would be very good to have the status on this. Being able to add blocks of hours is very important.

posts.author: festgebaeck (Tim Wilden)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know our team is working on adding the end time for date columns, which would allow you to choose blocks of time for specific tasks!

Maybe I missed it, but is there any update on this. Almost a year has gone by without the mention of end times either for date or timeline columns. Maybe something changed somehwere else to achieve this. But: Is there any news on this crucial feature?

posts.author: Rhyno3000 (Ryan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello All

Any update on this feature as well?

Regards

posts.author: eobrien12 (Eric)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello - adding the ‘end time’ to the date column would work wonderful for us also. Any update here?

thank you!

posts.author: Zahid (Zahid Malik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just to add to the above - this would really useful. Any update @brett-monday.com would be greatly appreciated. Would be happy to join an alpha.

Thank you.

posts.author: Julio (Tester)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, I come from the future to let you know we are still waiting for this critical feature.

posts.author: Julio (Tester)
posts.cookedHtml: @brett-monday.com Hi Bret, Our company has just joined to Monday and we are struggling to plan our projects without the ability to visualise in the timeline column any task that last less than a day. For example, I am working in 20 different task (each task last about 1.5 hours ) It would be extremely useful to visualize in the timeline all my task assuming a working day of 7.5 hours so I would be able to see I will be busy for the next 4 days, and if I get stuck in one task I could just move all my remaining task forward. For now we are trying to find a non elegant workaround (luckily we got several engineers that are helping), but, as Project Manager and Team Leader, I can confirm this is an important missing one.

posts.author: wamorim (William Nilson de Amorim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Two years and no updates about an obvious and highly requested feature. Customers are here taking their time to explain how important this feature is and not even a message to say “sorry, it is not in the plans for now”. Be transparent! Respect our time!

That’s why open-source software and services have so much space in the market.

posts.author: Julio (Tester)
posts.cookedHtml: Two years requesting this? really?. Our company just joined Monday, and as project manager I was hopping they move quickly (in particular for those request that make sense and are kind of obvious). Come on!, no one in Monday realised that some task requieres less than one day?. ugggggghhh. Does anyone know any other application that can achieve this? I don’t want to come back in two years time writing the same as you.

posts.author: Darby (Darby Bicheno)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, I really need this feature too. We would like to manage time on machines in our manufacturing facility. Often a task can run between 3-40 hours. I want to show in either the ‘workload view’ or the ‘timeline view’ exactly when a task is due to start and finish. Currently I have a numbers column showing the number of hours, and a timeline column for the dates it runs over, but I am unable to accurately show in the timeline when one task finishes and the next begins. I’m also unable to set max workload by the day, only the week. Ideally I would say each machine can run for maximum 24 hours a day, 6 days a week, to show clashes with the schedule.

Thanks, and eagerly awaiting this feature!

posts.author: BDixon999 (Barry Dixon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, do we know when this will likely hit the live systems…? I have been trying to create something similar but with zero success… Trying to create a cutover plan based on hourly tasks feeding into the main board; do not want to head back to Excel yet again…

Regards

Barry

Adding my voice to this too. Clickup has the feature. Is there an ETA?

posts.author: mattp.enterprise (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be good to:

  • Include minutes & hours in the column type for timeline

  • Add the this timeline as a variable to the Calendar view.

As you can see here. this calendar view allows for you to see when things are due and only does in 30 minute incruments. Having this timeline feature, could allow you to time block. You could allocate specific resources/time to the tasks and keep on track by having longer than 30 minutes. then the time tracking can then once you are working on it, tell you exactly how long it takes. And you can compare the diference. This is really good for planning.

image1499×642 28.3 KB

posts.author: KhurramAziz (Khurram Aziz)
posts.cookedHtml: Any further updates to this? I’m a new user and I can’t figure out a way to do this. We need to have about 3-4 hour time blocks in our workflow that need to be synced to a calendar (these are scheduled meetings) and I haven’t found a good way to create this.

posts.author: FNL (Francis Nadeau Lussier)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brett-monday.com, @Julia-monday.com,

Any news on this request? Do we know if it’s on the roadmap? What can we expect?

I’m currently trying to find workarounds with different external calendars integrations but it’s not good enough for the teams I’m trying to integrate with. It’s currently the only huge obstacle to adoption I have to deal with when trying to deploy Monday in all our teams.

We would really need this feature to see the light of day on Monday’s side (and not through integrations to external calendars). I believe I could make it work either through timelines or date columns but we would at least need one of the two options.

I assume that the feature must be very complicated to develop and I understand that there are not that many votes tied to this ongoing thread but I believe that everyone could benefit from a feature like this one.

Could we at least get an idea of where Monday as a company stands on this particular feature?

Thanks!

I also need to plan bigger projects in between those urgent recurring jobs and monitor overall workload of staff. not having these show up as timeblocks in the calendar is a nightmare. (or being able to rearange by the hour in workload view)
I consider swithing to clickup just because of this one (obvious) feature. Nobody in our industry plans in days, timelines should have the option to start and end on set time(hour) not only days.

Please, please add this feature.

posts.author: robbo1 (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml: Im struggling to understand the difficulty of implementing this change. I doubt I’ll renew my subscription if this isn’t resolved.

Please consider adding this.

For our nonprofit there is a work section I am creating that is absolutely essential that has timelines that allow both widget and assigment to be in hours - since it is a single-day event management schedule, and putting the activities within the day makes no benefit whatsoever.

Is there any other method in the meantime like a plugin or integration that can do this but still show within monday.com?

posts.cookedHtml: Hi @KCordaro i am merging this with another similar request so that the votes can get counted together! Make sure to vote on the item as well!

Has anyone found a solution for this?

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re revamping the timeline column and we want your help!

My name is Amber and I am part of the team responsible for all the columns you see on your monday Boards We’re looking for highly engaged users to shape the future of our timeline column! As a design partner in this journey, you’ll collaborate closely with our product and design teams over the next few weeks and then get early access to the functionality that you helped design!

We’re looking for dedicated users who are excited to provide feedback and work with us to bring this vision to life. If this sounds like you, please fill out this form

posts.author: johnbijl (John Bijl)
posts.cookedHtml: Let me add another voice to the call for hourly timelines! The timeline-function with just days is useless in out work. I like Monday for our project management, but for planning our meetings and lectures (that’s what we do) it is useless.

posts.author: anowacka (Anna from SolDevelo)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this?

posts.author: colincsimmons (Colin Simmons)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my voice to the throng! @amber Any updates on this feature? I want to use Monday for an event agenda, ideally, we’d have down to the minute functionality

posts.author: preetcharansingh (Preetcharan Singh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Syncing to Google Calendar is a NIGHTMARE.

I want to sync items and subitems (which are events we’ve booked) to google calendar, and it is so hard to do that if you only want to sync specfic items based on status. I had to use a work around where when an event is booked, assign someone to a column, so i had to make a new random account and do that, then i was able to use the automation when someone is assigned then sync to google, but the problem is if that event is cancelled, and you change the status, or remove the person or even archive the item, the event still remains on google calendar,

so i was thinking well what about a timeline sync, but that doesn’t even have times, so on google calendar which our speakers would use to know go to their event won’t even know what time it’s at…

Please monday sort this out!

posts.author: OttomanBeds (OttomanBeds)
posts.cookedHtml: Including hours in timeline selection allows for more precise scheduling and tracking, especially for tasks or events that require hourly planning rather than just daily or weekly overviews. It helps users see detailed time blocks, manage deadlines, and coordinate activities efficiently, making project management or personal planning more accurate and actionable.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Glenda! This has been a long-standing limitation of the Timeline column - it handles days well but breaks down completely when you need true start/end times within the same day. For use cases like interviews, field work, on-site visits, or anything where staff safety and scheduling order matter, the current timeline granularity just isn’t enough.

If you need a practical workaround right now, Time in Status for monday.com can help you track the actual time spent on each task or visit, including start/end times and duration. It doesn’t replace a proper hour-level timeline column, but it does give you reliable time-based reporting and per-item time ranges that monday doesn’t natively provide.
